title: olearia ciliata
intro: Olearia ciliata, also known as the hairy tree daisy, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Olearia ciliata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
